title: Lemon Chiffon Cake Day: Light, Fluffy Fun!
story: Every March 29th, bakers across America celebrate National Lemon Chiffon Cake Day by whipping up this special dessert! Chiffon cake is super light and airy because it's made with oil instead of butter, plus beaten egg whites that make it fluffy like a cloud. People love making lemon chiffon cake because it tastes bright and citrusy, perfect for spring. Bakeries sell extra slices on this day, and home bakers share photos of their yellow masterpieces online. The best part? This cake is so soft and spongy, it practically melts in your mouth!
activity_question: What makes chiffon cake so light and fluffy?
activity_choices: Oil instead of butter and beaten egg whites
Baking it in a pizza oven
Adding fizzy soda to the batter
activity_answer: Oil instead of butter and beaten egg whites
bonus_fact: Chiffon cake was invented in 1927 but kept secret for 20 years!

title: Smoke and Mirrors Day: Magic or Trickery?
story: March 29th is Smoke and Mirrors Day, when people celebrate the art of illusion and magic tricks! Magicians perform special shows using classic "smoke and mirrors" techniques to amaze audiences. Kids practice card tricks, coin disappearing acts, and simple illusions they can share with friends and family. The phrase "smoke and mirrors" also means when someone tries to confuse or trick others, so some people use this day to learn about spotting fake news or misleading information online. Libraries and schools often host magic workshops where children can learn the secrets behind famous tricks and create their own magical performances.
activity_question: What do magicians traditionally use to create illusions on Smoke and Mirrors Day?
follow_up_question: What's the coolest magic trick you've ever seen or would like to learn?

title: When Niagara Falls Forgot to Flow
story: Every March 29th, people celebrate one of the weirdest days in waterfall history! On this day in 1848, Niagara Falls actually stopped flowing for about 30 hours. An ice jam blocked the Niagara River, and suddenly the thundering water went silent. People walked out onto the dry riverbed and picked up fish and artifacts! Today, visitors to Niagara Falls on March 29th love sharing photos and stories about this incredible day when nature hit the pause button. Tour guides tell the amazing tale, and people imagine what it would be like to see the famous falls completely dry again.
activity_question: What caused Niagara Falls to stop flowing in 1848?
activity_choices: An ice jam blocked the river
Someone forgot to pay the water bill
activity_answer: An ice jam blocked the river
bonus_fact: People found old guns, tomahawks, and other artifacts in the dry riverbed!
